zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Handlo於1333年9月5日出生在英國英格蘭肯特時，她的父親Sir John Hadlow64歲，母親Maud Burnell43歲。她於1366年5月20日與John De Appleby在英國英格蘭諾福爾可提爾尼 聖 勞倫斯結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1371年12月在英國享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Handlo于1333年9月5日出生在英国英格兰肯特时，她的父亲Sir John Hadlow64岁，母亲Maud Burnell43岁。她于1366年5月20日与John De Appleby在英国英格兰諾福爾可提爾尼 聖 勞倫斯结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1371年12月在英国享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Handlo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Hanley  .

variants.name: Hanley
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conor, Declan, Donovan, Eamonn, John Patrick.

Irish: shortened form of O'Hanley, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁinle ‘descendant of Áinle’, a personal name meaning ‘champion’. This is the name of a former ruling family in Connacht and is now common throughout southern Ireland.

English: habitational name from any of the many places called Handley (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Dorset, Northamptonshire) or Hanley (Staffordshire, Worcestershire), named with Old English hēan, the weak dative form (originally used after a preposition and article) of hēah ‘high’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Handlo was born on 5 September 1333, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Hadlow, was 64 and her mother, Maud Burnell, was 43. She married John De Appleby on 20 May 1366, in Tilney St Lawrence, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in December 1371, in England, at the age of 38.

Elle a épousé John De Appleby le 20 mai 1366, à Tilney St Lawrence, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en décembre 1371, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Handlo(이)가 1333년 9월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Hadlow(은)는 64세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Burnell(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1366년 5월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 틸네이 스트 로렌스에서 John De Appleby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1371년 12월 영국에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Handlo родилась 5 сентября 1333 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John Hadlow было 64 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Burnell, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John De Appleby 20 мая 1366 г. в местности тилни ст ларенс, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла декабрь 1371 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 38 лет.
